name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Signature Algorithm Does Not Match To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm
notes: This rule triggers when an X509 certificate has been observed with a Signature Algorithm that does not match the To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm. This may be considered a suspicious connection.
conditions_text: when any of Suspect Content Descriptions match Signature Algorithm does not match To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Signature Algorithm Does Not Match To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when any of Suspect Content Descriptions match Signature Algorithm does not match To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Signature Algorithm Does Not Match To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm
Event description: Detects when an X509 certificate has been observed with a Signature Algorithm that does not match the To-Be-Signed Signature Algorithm.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
